frame_analyses.description: Instructor in lab coat and surgical attire is shown; equipment laid out on table including FLS trainer box, needle drivers, scissors, knot pusher, Penrose drain on block, and suture materials
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Educational setting with explicit labeling of task (TASK-16) visible on printed materials

frame_analyses.description: Hands grasping pink foam Penrose drain positioned on suture block; suture visible with white/translucent coloring; needle driver beginning to manipulate drain
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Drain is properly secured on block; two operators visible (instructor demonstration with trainee assistance or collaborative setup)

frame_analyses.description: Penrose drain held firmly in left hand; right hand with needle driver positioned to approach marked targets on drain; suture line visible entering drain region
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Drain stabilization appears adequate; target marks on drain visible but resolution limits precise mark identification

frame_analyses.description: Needle passing through first marked target on Penrose drain; suture thread visible running through drain tissue; entry point appears close to marked location
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Perpendicular needle entry angle observed; proper drive mechanics

frame_analyses.description: Suture needle completing passage through second marked target; both entry and exit points now visible on drain; suture thread completely threaded through both marks
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Distance between two passes appears consistent with task requirements; suture sits cleanly through drain

frame_analyses.description: Hands manipulating suture extracorporeally outside the trainer; first throw being formed; suture ends controlled with both hands to create loop
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Clean loop formation visible; appropriate tension management; hand positioning demonstrates control

frame_analyses.description: Knot pusher instrument visible engaging the first throw and advancing it down toward drain; knot sliding smoothly along suture; drain remains stable
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Pusher technique appears smooth; knot advancing without catching or slipping

frame_analyses.description: Second throw being formed outside trainer; suture ends manipulated to create second loop; rhythm and hand position consistent with throw 1
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Alternating hand lead apparent; controlled knot formation

frame_analyses.description: Knot pusher advancing second throw down suture toward drain; two knot loops now visible on suture approaching the drain tissue
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Pusher control maintained; knot seating appropriately

frame_analyses.description: Third throw being formed; suture ends again controlled to create final loop; drain begins to show signs of slit approximation as knots approach
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Final throw mechanics consistent with previous throws; appropriate tension observed

frame_analyses.description: Final knot pusher advancement; third throw advancing to drain; drain slit appears to close under knot tension; all three knots now seated against drain
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Final cinching visible; drain slit approximation achieved

frame_analyses.description: Endoscopic scissors positioned to cut suture ends; both suture tails cut cleanly inside trainer (evidenced by final frame showing completed knot on drain with tails removed)
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Clean scissor cuts; suture ends fully cut inside trainer as required

cannot_determine: Exact completion time — video is instructional/educational demonstration with multiple pauses, narration/explanation implied by setup and instructor presence; does not show continuous real-time performance
Precise mm deviation at marks — camera angles are oblique and resolution insufficient to measure entry/exit points to 1mm accuracy
Whether tension test was performed — video ends after cutting without explicit final drain integrity check under tension shown
video_classification_rationale: This is an **instructional video**. Key indicators: (1) Instructor in full lab attire visible in opening frames, not performing; (2) Equipment is meticulously laid out and labeled with printed task cards and center branding visible; (3) Multiple camera angles and still-frame composition suggest educational documentation rather than real-time exam/proficiency testing; (4) Deliberate pacing and frame transitions suggest narration or educational structure; (5) No timer or clock visible; (6) Collaborative hand positioning (two operators visible in many frames) indicates teaching demonstration rather than solo performance evaluation. **Per rubric rules, instructional videos are NOT scored**; teaching content is extracted instead.

demonstrated_techniques: Proper drain stabilization on suture block using non-dominant hand
Perpendicular needle entry angle for both target passes
Controlled suture threading through marked targets with clean path
Extracorporeal knot formation using proper hand mechanics — loop creation with tension control
Knot pusher technique: smooth advancement along suture without catching or bunching
Sequential three-throw knot tying with consistent hand position and tension
Final cinching of knot against drain to achieve slit closure
Complete suture tail removal by cutting inside trainer after knot is secure
verbal_cues: Task label clearly visible: 'TASK - 16' with center branding 'TVR LAPAROSCOPY CENTER'
Setup frames emphasize equipment organization and accessibility
common_errors_addressed: Proper drain stabilization prevents slipping during needle passes
Perpendicular entry angle avoids shallow passes and reduces deviation
Controlled extracorporeal knot formation prevents loose or asymmetrical throws
Smooth pusher technique prevents knot jamming or tearing
Adequate final tension ensures slit closure without overtightening that could avulse drain
expert_indicators: Smooth, efficient hand transitions between throw formations
Knot pusher advancement is controlled and direct — no hesitation or repositioning
Drain remains stable throughout all passes and cinching
No visible suture catching or tangling
Clean, complete slit closure achieved by final throw
Timing and rhythm suggest mastery of the task sequence

rationale_for_no_score: Per FLS proctor rubric rules, instructional videos with explicit educational structure (visible instructor, labeled materials, collaborative demonstration setup, multi-angle camera work) are not scored. This video exhibits all markers of educational/tutorial content designed to teach the task rather than evaluate a trainee's or surgeon's proficiency. A performance or expert_demo video would show a single operator performing the task continuously with a visible timer or formal exam environment.
